programme.title: Mad Men
programme.desc: Duck offers Pete a promotion, Betty has a dangerous liaison after receiving some unwanted news, and Don returns.

programme.title: Mad Men
programme.desc: Don accidentally learns a secret about Salvatore during a business trip to Baltimore; Back at the office, the firings continue, but the new management appoints Peter and Ken as new co-heads of accounts.

programme.title: Mad Men
programme.desc: Betty's father visits; Sterling Cooper handles a very specific client request; Roger makes wedding arrangements; Peggy is affected personally by a campaign.

programme.title: Mad Men
programme.desc: The writers are forced to work after hours; Roger hosts a party; Joan and Greg host their own party; Sally has a run-in with Grandpa.

programme.title: Mad Men
programme.desc: Don has reservations about a misguided potential client who wants to sell jai alai as the `new national pastime'; Betty's father passes away.

programme.title: Mad Men
programme.desc: Betty has hallucinations of her dead father as she delivers her baby; Peggy begins to question her future at Sterling Cooper.

programme.title: Mad Men
programme.sub-title: Guy Walks Into an Advertising Agency
programme.desc: The new British bosses want the staff to come in to meet their dashing new blade Guy MacKendrick; Joan tenders her resignation only to find that she has been too rash.

programme.title: BlackBerry: The Limited Series
programme.desc: Mike Lazaridis and Doug Fregin pitch their idea of combining a computer with a cellphone to Jim Balsillie; Jim comes on board, and they try to sell their idea.

programme.title: BlackBerry: The Limited Series
programme.desc: BlackBerry hits the market; Mike and Jim try to avoid a hostile takeover; Jim strategizes to increase sales.

programme.title: BlackBerry: The Limited Series
programme.desc: Apple announces the iPhone; Mike scrambles to please Verizon; Jim is unreachable trying to make a personal business deal; the Securities and Exchange Commission investigates the company.

programme.title: Black Snow: Jack of Clubs
programme.desc: In 2003 Zoe Jacobs disappeared during her 21st birthday party; many believe Zoe ran away but others suspect she was murdered; when police officer Sam Kahlil discovers Zoe's backpack, Detective James Cormack is called in to investigate.

programme.title: Black Snow: Jack of Clubs
programme.desc: New evidence gives Nadja and Sam hope that Zoe is still alive, but as Cormack puts together a timeline of Zoe's disappearance, the old theory that Zoe drove to Brisbane doesn't add up.

programme.title: Black Snow: Jack of Clubs
programme.desc: Cormack invites Sam to examine the contents of Zoe's backpack and probe her memory for clues; re-examining CCTV footage from the night she disappeared, he discovers Zoe actually drove to Xanadu, the rural property where Joseph was living.

programme.title: Black Snow: Jack of Clubs
programme.desc: A tragic discovery sends shock waves through the community, particularly for those clinging to hope that Zoe was still alive; Sam's anonymous phone calls are officially traced; Cormack turns his attention to Joseph.

programme.title: Black Snow: Jack of Clubs
programme.desc: The field of suspects narrows; Sean's computer reveals a history of despicable behavior towards women, including the photos taken of Zoe without her consent.

programme.title: Black Snow: Jack of Clubs
programme.desc: Cormack is hot on the heels of Zoe's killer after Julie is thrust into the spotlight; it turns out that Leo had been lying about his alibi all along.

programme.title: Once Upon a Time in Venice
programme.desc: To get his dog back, private eye Steve Ford forges an unlikely alliance with Spyder, the leader of a local gang. With help from his best friend, Steve pulls out the big guns to retrieve Spyder's missing cash and cocaine and save his beloved pooch.

programme.title: The Audacity
programme.desc: In a world of jaded billionaires, psychiatrist-gurus, bio-hacked tech bros, AI labs and disillusioned teens being optimized in elite private schools, an audacious data-mining CEO strives to turn insight and influence into profit and power.

programme.title: Tomorrow You're Gone
programme.desc: A mysterious woman (Michelle Monaghan) helps an ex-convict (Stephen Dorff) after a planned murder goes wrong.

programme.title: Mad Men
programme.desc: As Don secures an account with Conrad Hilton, Sterling and Cooper try to strong-arm Don into signing a contract; Betty meets with her acquaintance from the governor's office.

programme.title: Mad Men
programme.desc: Don and Betty renew their love affair while on a business trip to Rome; Peter has a bout of infidelity with an au pair in his building while his wife is away.

programme.title: Mad Men
programme.desc: Betty tries to meet with Henry Francis again; Don steps up his pursuit of his daughter's former teacher. Don disappoints Conrad Hilton and is forced by a client to fire Salvatore over an unwelcome advance.
